title: podman crashes if `required` mount flag is used
body:
### Issue Description

podman crashes when attempting to build an image from Dockerfile where `required` mount flag is used.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a Dockerfile:
```
FROM docker.io/nginx:1.23.3

RUN --mount=type=secret,id=nginx-crt,dst=/nginx-repo.crt,required \
    --mount=type=secret,id=nginx-key,dst=/nginx-repo.key,required \
    set -x \
    && . /etc/os-release \
    && stat /nginx-repo.crt \
    && stat /nginx-repo.key
```

2. Run `podman build`:
```
% podman build -t defanator:1 --secret id=nginx-crt,src=nginx-repo.crt,dst=/nginx-repo.crt --secret id=nginx-key,src=nginx-repo.key,dst=/nginx-repo.key - < docker/Dockerfile.example
STEP 1/2: FROM docker.io/nginx:1.23.3
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=secret,id=nginx-crt,dst=/nginx-repo.crt,required     --mount=type=secret,id=nginx-key,dst=/nginx-repo.key,required     set -x     && . /etc/os-release     && stat /nginx-repo.crt     && stat /nginx-repo.key
Error: server probably quit: unexpected EOF
```

### Describe the results you received

No image created, podman machine logs:
```
May 03 17:06:28 localhost.localdomain systemd[1]: Started systemd-coredump@17-5399-0.service - Process Core Dump (PID 5399/UID 0).
May 03 17:06:29 localhost.localdomain systemd-coredump[5400]: [🡕] Process 5367 (podman) of user 502 dumped core.

                                                              Module podman from rpm podman-4.5.0-1.fc38.aarch64

                                                              #0  0x0000aaaac187d658 runtime.raise.abi0 (podman + 0x49d658)

                                                              #3  0x0000aaaac185d9bc runtime.sigtrampgo (podman + 0x47d9bc)
                                                              #4  0x0000aaaac187da24 runtime.sigtramp.abi0 (podman + 0x49da24)
                                                              #5  0x0000ffffba6207fc n/a (linux-vdso.so.1 + 0x7fc)
                                                              #6  0x0000aaaac185e9a4 runtime.dieFromSignal (podman + 0x47e9a4)
                                                              #7  0x0000aaaac18478b8 runtime.fatalpanic (podman + 0x4678b8)

                                                              #10 0x0000aaaac2418290 github.com/containers/podman/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah.(*Builder).getSecretMount (podman + 0x1038290)
                                                              #11 0x0000aaaac24166b0 github.com/containers/podman/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah.(*Builder).runSetupRunMounts (podman + 0x10366b0)
                                                              #12 0x0000aaaac2413f74 github.com/containers/podman/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah.(*Builder).setupMounts (podman + 0x1033f74)
                                                              #13 0x0000aaaac241b550 github.com/containers/podman/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah.(*Builder).Run (podman + 0x103b550)
                                                              #14 0x0000aaaac247b6b4 github.com/containers/podman/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/imagebuildah.(*StageExecutor).Run (podman + 0x109b6b4)
                                                              #15 0x0000aaaac239a3f4 github.com/containers/podman/vendor/github.com/openshift/imagebuilder.(*Builder).Run (podman + 0xfba3f4)
                                                              #16 0x0000aaaac247f8dc github.com/containers/podman/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/imagebuildah.(*StageExecutor).Execute (podman + 0x109f8dc)
                                                              ELF object binary architecture: AARCH64
May 03 17:06:29 localhost.localdomain systemd[1817]: podman.service: Main process exited, code=dumped, status=6/ABRT
May 03 17:06:29 localhost.localdomain systemd[1817]: podman.service: Failed with result 'core-dump'.
```

### Describe the results you expected

Image created without errors.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Running with podman 4.5.0 from homebrew on macOS 12.6.3:
```
% sw_vers 
ProductName:	macOS
ProductVersion:	12.6.3
BuildVersion:	21G419
```

podman machine version:
```
[core@localhost log]$ cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="38.20230430.2.1 (CoreOS)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=38
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f38"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora CoreOS 38.20230430.2.1"
ANSI_COLOR="0;38;2;60;110;180"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:38"
HOME_URL="https://getfedora.org/coreos/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora-coreos/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker/"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=38
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=38
SUPPORT_END=2024-05-14
VARIANT="CoreOS"
VARIANT_ID=coreos
OSTREE_VERSION='38.20230430.2.1'
```

### Additional information

Removing `required` mount option fixes the issue:
```
% diff -u docker/Dockerfile.example docker/Dockerfile.example2
--- docker/Dockerfile.example	2023-05-03 17:03:04.000000000 +0400
+++ docker/Dockerfile.example2	2023-05-03 17:05:06.000000000 +0400
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 FROM docker.io/nginx:1.23.3
 
-RUN --mount=type=secret,id=nginx-crt,dst=/nginx-repo.crt,required \
-    --mount=type=secret,id=nginx-key,dst=/nginx-repo.key,required \
+RUN --mount=type=secret,id=nginx-crt,dst=/nginx-repo.crt \
+    --mount=type=secret,id=nginx-key,dst=/nginx-repo.key \
     set -x \
     && . /etc/os-release \
     && stat /nginx-repo.crt \

% podman build --no-cache -t defanator:1 --secret id=nginx-crt,src=nginx-repo.crt,dst=/nginx-repo.crt --secret id=nginx-key,src=nginx-repo.key,dst=/nginx-repo.key - < docker/Dockerfile.example2
STEP 1/2: FROM docker.io/nginx:1.23.3
STEP 2/2: RUN --mount=type=secret,id=nginx-crt,dst=/nginx-repo.crt     --mount=type=secret,id=nginx-key,dst=/nginx-repo.key     set -x     && . /etc/os-release     && stat /nginx-repo.crt     && stat /nginx-repo.key
+ . /etc/os-release
+ PRETTY_NAME=Debian GNU/Linux 11 (bullseye)
+ NAME=Debian GNU/Linux
+ VERSION_ID=11
+ VERSION=11 (bullseye)
+ VERSION_CODENAME=bullseye
+ ID=debian
+ HOME_URL=https://www.debian.org/
+ SUPPORT_URL=https://www.debian.org/support
+ BUG_REPORT_URL=https://bugs.debian.org/
+ stat /nginx-repo.crt
  File: /nginx-repo.crt
  Size: 1229      	Blocks: 8          IO Block: 4096   regular file
Device: fc04h/64516d	Inode: 135185143   Links: 1
Access: (0400/-r--------)  Uid: (    0/    root)   Gid: (    0/    root)
Access: 2023-05-03 13:12:09.586461680 +0000
Modify: 2023-05-03 13:12:09.586461680 +0000
Change: 2023-05-03 13:12:09.586461680 +0000

+ stat /nginx-repo.key

 Birth: 2023-05-03 13:12:09.586461680 +0000
COMMIT defanator:1
--> 6a96f7f50236
Successfully tagged localhost/defanator:1
6a96f7f5023616f3388564aabad24ae52faa06c17dd31ebc7fffdd89f1671c69
```
